tweets.text:
Most most most importantly - please backchannel your investors - especially if you have leverage. I’m tired of fielding calls with founders who have bad actors on their board. 

You can’t fire a board member. You can take a day to make sure it’s the right one!!

Here’s some advice most VCs won’t tell you directly when you’re raising funds as an early stage startup. 

Probably helpful for YC founders as you go into pitchapalooza next week.. 

> first, never monologue. A VCs attention span is worse than a toddler. trust me I have one and I
Quick addendum: I can primarily help if you are in the US and for non crypto/defense companies. I’ll try to get to all of you that I can help tonight!

tweets.text:
guys autoresearch is kind of amazing

it’s also very bitter lesson-pilled:

do away with all of your fancy agent infrastructure. just design the simplest possible system to let you throw more tokens at your problem
@thsottiaux you might find interesting

oh also for workflow, i use pins to keep things that i've worked on that haven't been deployed. so this is the list of ongoing work—anything that's finished gets unpinned

ends up being super messy. ideally the orchestrator also takes care of this

tweets.text:
One of the interesting aspects of seeing the AI-pilled knowledge workers is to see how much *fun* they are having.

It's not surprising -- it is fun to be in flow and to have a sense of solving interesting problems at a higher level of abstraction.
